values.name: all
values.comment:
Used with a Prism, think of `!!` boolean coercion in Javascript except type safe.

    Just "Stuff"
        |> all just_
    --> ["Stuff"]

    Nothing
        |> all just_
    --> []
values.type: (Accessors.Optic pr ls a b a b -> Accessors.Optic pr ls s t a b) -> s -> List.List a

values.name: at
values.comment:
at: Structure Preserving accessor over List members.
alias for [`List.Accessors.at`](List-Accessors#at)
values.type: Basics.Int -> Accessors.Optic pr ls a a x y -> Accessors.Traversal (List.List a) (List.List a) x y

values.name: each
values.comment:
This accessor combinator lets you access values inside List.
alias for [`List.Accessors.each`](List-Accessors#each)
values.type: Accessors.Optic pr ls a b x y -> Accessors.Traversal (List.List a) (List.List b) x y

values.name: eachIdx
values.comment:
This accessor lets you traverse a list including the index of each element
alias for [`List.Accessors.each_`](List-Accessors#each_)
values.type: Accessors.Optic pr ls ( Basics.Int, b ) c x y -> Accessors.Traversal (List.List b) (List.List c) x y

values.name: err_
values.type: Accessors.Optic pr ls a b x y -> Accessors.Prism pr (Result.Result a ignored) (Result.Result b ignored) x y

values.name: every
values.type: Accessors.Optic pr ls a b x y -> Accessors.Traversal (Array.Array a) (Array.Array b) x y

values.name: everyIdx
values.type: Accessors.Optic pr ls ( Basics.Int, b ) c x y -> Accessors.Traversal (Array.Array b) (Array.Array c) x y

values.name: from
values.comment: Flip an Isomorphism
values.type: (Accessors.Optic pr ls a b a b -> Accessors.Iso pr ls s t a b) -> Accessors.Optic pr ls t s t s -> Accessors.Iso pr ls b a t s

values.name: fst
values.comment:
Lens over the first component of a Tuple
alias for [`Tuple.Accessors.fst`](Tuple-Accessors#fst)

    import Accessors exposing (..)

    charging : (String, Int)
    charging = ("It's over", 1)

    get fst charging
    --> "It's over"

    set fst "It's over" charging
    --> ("It's over", 1)

    map fst (\s -> String.toUpper s ++ "!!!") charging
    --> ("IT'S OVER!!!", 1)
values.type: Accessors.Optic pr ls a b x y -> Accessors.Lens ls ( a, two ) ( b, two ) x y

values.name: get
values.comment:
The get function takes:

  - An accessor,
  - A datastructure with type `super`
    and returns the value accessed by that combinator.

```
get (foo << bar) myRecord
```
values.type: (Accessors.Optic pr ls a b a b -> Accessors.Optic pr () s t a b) -> s -> a

values.name: has
values.comment:
Used with a Prism, think of `!!` boolean coercion in Javascript except type safe.

    Just 1234
        |> has just_
    --> True

    Nothing
        |> has just_
    --> False

    [ "Wooo", "Things" ]
        |> has (at 7)
    --> False

    [ "Wooo", "Things" ]
        |> has (at 0)
    --> True
values.type: (Accessors.Optic pr ls a b a b -> Accessors.Optic pr ls s t a b) -> s -> Basics.Bool

values.name: iso
values.comment: An isomorphism constructor.
values.type: String.String -> (s -> a) -> (b -> t) -> Accessors.Optic pr ls a b x y -> Accessors.Iso pr ls s t x y

values.name: ix
values.comment:
alias for [`Array.Accessors.at`](Array-Accessors#at)

    import Accessors exposing (..)
    import Array exposing (Array)
    import Lens as L

    arr : Array { bar : String }
    arr = Array.fromList [{ bar = "Stuff" }, { bar =  "Things" }, { bar = "Woot" }]

    try (ix 1) arr
    --> Just { bar = "Things" }

    try (ix 9000) arr
    --> Nothing

    try (ix 0 << L.bar) arr
    --> Just "Stuff"

    set (ix 0 << L.bar) "Whatever" arr
    --> Array.fromList [{ bar = "Whatever" }, { bar =  "Things" }, { bar = "Woot" }]

    set (ix 9000 << L.bar) "Whatever" arr
    --> arr
values.type: Basics.Int -> Accessors.Optic pr ls a a x y -> Accessors.Traversal (Array.Array a) (Array.Array a) x y

values.name: ixd
values.comment: Lift an optic over an indexed traversal
values.type: (Accessors.Optic pr ls a b a b -> Accessors.Optic pr ls s t a b) -> Accessors.Optic pr ls a b x y -> Accessors.Traversal ( ix, s ) t x y

values.name: just_
values.type: Accessors.Optic pr ls a b x y -> Accessors.Prism pr (Maybe.Maybe a) (Maybe.Maybe b) x y

values.name: key
values.comment:
key: NON-structure preserving accessor over Dict's
alias for [`Dict.Accessors.at`](Dict-Accessors#at)

In terms of accessors, think of Dicts as records where each field is a Maybe.

    import Dict exposing (Dict)
    import Accessors exposing (..)
    import Lens as L

    dict : Dict String {bar : Int}
    dict = Dict.fromList [("foo", {bar = 2})]

    get (key "foo") dict
    --> Just {bar = 2}

    get (key "baz") dict
    --> Nothing

    try (key "foo" << just_ << L.bar) dict
    --> Just 2

    set (key "foo") Nothing dict
    --> Dict.remove "foo" dict

    set (key "baz" << just_ << L.bar) 3 dict
    --> dict
values.type: String.String -> Accessors.Optic pr ls (Maybe.Maybe a) (Maybe.Maybe a) x y -> Accessors.Lens ls (Dict.Dict String.String a) (Dict.Dict String.String a) x y

values.name: keyI
values.comment:
key: NON-structure preserving accessor over Dict's
alias for [`Dict.Accessors.id`](Dict-Accessors#id)

In terms of accessors, think of Dicts as records where each field is a Maybe.

    import Dict exposing (Dict)
    import Accessors exposing (..)
    import Lens as L

    dict : Dict Int {bar : Int}
    dict = Dict.fromList [(1, {bar = 2})]

    get (keyI 1) dict
    --> Just {bar = 2}

    get (keyI 0) dict
    --> Nothing

    try (keyI 1 << just_ << L.bar) dict
    --> Just 2

    set (keyI 1) Nothing dict
    --> Dict.remove 1 dict

    set (keyI 0 << just_ << L.bar) 3 dict
    --> dict
values.type: Basics.Int -> Accessors.Optic pr ls (Maybe.Maybe a) (Maybe.Maybe a) x y -> Accessors.Lens ls (Dict.Dict Basics.Int a) (Dict.Dict Basics.Int a) x y

values.name: key_
values.comment:
`key_`: NON-structure preserving accessor over Dict's
alias for [`Dict.Accessors.at_`](Dict-Accessors#at_)

In terms of accessors, think of Dicts as records where each field is a Maybe.

    import Dict exposing (Dict)
    import Accessors exposing (..)
    import Lens as L

    dict : Dict Char {bar : Int}
    dict = Dict.fromList [('C', {bar = 2})]

    keyC : Char -> Optic pr ls (Maybe {bar : Int}) (Maybe {bar : Int}) x y -> Lens ls (Dict Char {bar : Int}) (Dict Char {bar : Int}) x y
    keyC =
        key_ String.fromChar

    get (keyC 'C') dict
    --> Just {bar = 2}

    get (keyC 'Z') dict
    --> Nothing

    try (keyC 'C' << just_ << L.bar) dict
    --> Just 2

    set (keyC 'C') Nothing dict
    --> Dict.remove 'C' dict

    set (keyC 'Z' << just_ << L.bar) 3 dict
    --> dict
values.type: (comparable -> String.String) -> comparable -> Accessors.Optic pr ls (Maybe.Maybe a) (Maybe.Maybe a) x y -> Accessors.Lens ls (Dict.Dict comparable a) (Dict.Dict comparable a) x y

values.name: keyed
values.type: Accessors.Optic pr ls ( a, b ) c x y -> Accessors.Traversal (Dict.Dict a b) (Dict.Dict a c) x y

values.name: lens
values.comment:
This exposes a description field that's necessary for use with the name function
for getting unique names out of compositions of accessors. This is useful when you
want type safe keys for a Dictionary but you still want to use elm/core implementation.

    foo : Optic attr view over -> Optic { rec | foo : attr } view over
    foo =
        makeOneToOne
            ".foo"
            .foo
            (\change rec -> { rec | foo = change rec.foo })
values.type: String.String -> (s -> a) -> (s -> b -> t) -> Accessors.Optic pr ls a b x y -> Accessors.Lens ls s t x y

values.name: map
values.comment:
The over function takes:

  - An accessor,
  - A function `(sub -> sub)`,
  - A datastructure with type `super`
    and it returns the data structure, with the accessible field changed by applying
    the function to the existing value.

```
map (foo << qux) ((+) 1) myRecord
```
values.type: (Accessors.Optic pr ls a b a b -> Accessors.Optic pr ls s t a b) -> (a -> b) -> s -> t

values.name: name
values.comment: This function gives the name of the function as a string...
values.type: (Accessors.Optic pr ls a b a b -> Accessors.Optic pr ls s t a b) -> String.String

values.name: new
values.comment: Use prism to reconstruct.
values.type: (Accessors.Optic pr ls a b a b -> Accessors.Optic () ls s t a b) -> b -> t

values.name: ok_
values.type: Accessors.Optic pr ls a b x y -> Accessors.Prism pr (Result.Result ignored a) (Result.Result ignored b) x y

values.name: prism
values.comment:
A prism constructor.

Parameters are: reconstructor and a splitter.

Reconstructor takes a final value and constructs a final object.

The splitter turns initial object either to final object directly (if initial object is of wrong variant),
or spits out `a`.
values.type: String.String -> (b -> t) -> (s -> Result.Result t a) -> Accessors.Optic pr ls a b x y -> Accessors.Prism pr s t x y

values.name: set
values.comment:
The set function takes:

  - An accessor,
  - A value of the type `sub`,
  - A datastructure with type `super`
    and it returns the data structure, with the accessible field changed to be
    the set value.

```
set (foo << bar) "Hi!" myRecord
```
values.type: (Accessors.Optic pr ls a b a b -> Accessors.Optic pr ls s t a b) -> b -> s -> t

values.name: snd
values.comment:
alias for [`Tuple.Accessors.snd`](Tuple-Accessors#snd)

    import Accessors exposing (..)

    meh : (String, Int)
    meh = ("It's over", 1)

    get snd meh
    --> 1

    set snd 1125 meh
    --> ("It's over", 1125)

    meh
        |> set snd 1125
        |> map fst (\s -> String.toUpper s ++ "!!!")
        |> map snd ((*) 8)
    --> ("IT'S OVER!!!", 9000)
values.type: Accessors.Optic pr ls a b x y -> Accessors.Lens ls ( one, a ) ( one, b ) x y

values.name: traversal
values.comment:
This exposes a description field that's necessary for use with the name function
for getting unique names out of compositions of accessors. This is useful when you
want type safe keys for a Dictionary but you still want to use elm/core implementation.

    each : Optic pr ls a b x y -> Traversal (List a) (List b) x y
    each =
        Base.traversal "[]"
            identity
            List.map
values.type: String.String -> (s -> List.List a) -> ((a -> b) -> s -> t) -> Accessors.Optic pr ls a b x y -> Accessors.Traversal s t x y

values.name: try
values.comment:
Used with a Prism, think of `!!` boolean coercion in Javascript except type safe.

    ["Stuff", "things"]
        |> try (at 2)
    --> Nothing

    ["Stuff", "things"]
        |> try (at 0)
    --> Just "Stuff"
values.type: (Accessors.Optic pr ls a b a b -> Accessors.Optic pr ls s t a b) -> s -> Maybe.Maybe a

values.name: values
values.type: Accessors.Optic pr ls a b x y -> Accessors.Traversal (Dict.Dict key a) (Dict.Dict key b) x y

values.name: at
values.comment:
This accessor combinator lets you access Array indices.
In terms of accessors, think of Dicts as records where each field is a Maybe.

    import Accessors exposing (..)
    import Array exposing (Array)
    import Array.Accessors as Array
    import Lens as L

    arr : Array { bar : String }
    arr = Array.fromList [{ bar = "Stuff" }, { bar = "Things" }, { bar = "Woot" }]

    try (Array.at 1) arr
    --> Just { bar = "Things" }

    try (Array.at 9000) arr
    --> Nothing

    try (Array.at 0 << L.bar) arr
    --> Just "Stuff"

    set (Array.at 0 << L.bar) "Whatever" arr
    --> Array.fromList [{ bar = "Whatever" }, { bar = "Things" }, { bar = "Woot" }]

    set (Array.at 9000 << L.bar) "Whatever" arr
    --> arr
values.type: Basics.Int -> Base.Optic pr ls a a x y -> Base.Traversal (Array.Array a) (Array.Array a) x y

values.name: each
values.type: Base.Optic pr ls a b x y -> Base.Traversal (Array.Array a) (Array.Array b) x y

values.name: each_
values.type: Base.Optic pr ls ( Basics.Int, b ) c x y -> Base.Traversal (Array.Array b) (Array.Array c) x y

values.name: id
values.comment:
This accessor combinator lets you access a record with a particular id in an Array.
In terms of accessors, think of Dicts as records where each field is a Maybe.

    import Accessors exposing (..)
    import Array exposing (Array)
    import Array.Accessors as Array
    import Lens as L

    arr : Array { id : Int, bar : String }
    arr = Array.fromList [{ id = 7, bar = "Stuff" }, { id = 1, bar = "Things" }, { id = 5, bar = "Woot" }]

    try (Array.id 1) arr
    --> Just { id = 1, bar = "Things" }

    try (Array.id 9000) arr
    --> Nothing

    try (Array.id 7 << L.bar) arr
    --> Just "Stuff"

    set (Array.id 7 << L.bar) "Whatever" arr
    --> Array.fromList [{ id = 7, bar = "Whatever" }, { id = 1, bar = "Things" }, { id = 5, bar = "Woot" }]

    set (Array.id 9000 << L.bar) "Whatever" arr
    --> arr
values.type: Basics.Int -> Base.Optic pr ls { m | id : Basics.Int } { m | id : Basics.Int } x y -> Base.Traversal (Array.Array { m | id : Basics.Int }) (Array.Array { m | id : Basics.Int }) x y

name: Dict.Accessors
comment:
Dict.Accessors

@docs each, each_, at, id, at_

values.name: at
values.comment:
at: NON-structure preserving accessor over Dict's

In terms of accessors, think of Dicts as records where each field is a Maybe.

    import Accessors exposing (..)
    import Dict exposing (Dict)
    import Dict.Accessors as Dict
    import Lens as L

    dict : Dict String {bar : Int}
    dict = Dict.fromList [("foo", {bar = 2})]

    get (Dict.at "foo") dict
    --> Just {bar = 2}

    get (Dict.at "baz") dict
    --> Nothing

    try (Dict.at "foo" << just_ << L.bar) dict
    --> Just 2

    set (Dict.at "foo") Nothing dict
    --> Dict.remove "foo" dict

    set (Dict.at "baz" << just_ << L.bar) 3 dict
    --> dict
values.type: String.String -> Base.Optic pr ls (Maybe.Maybe a) (Maybe.Maybe a) x y -> Base.Lens ls (Dict.Dict String.String a) (Dict.Dict String.String a) x y

values.name: at_
values.comment:
`at_`: NON-structure preserving accessor over Dict's

In terms of accessors, think of Dicts as records where each field is a Maybe.

    import Accessors exposing (..)
    import Dict exposing (Dict)
    import Dict.Accessors as Dict
    import Lens as L

    dict : Dict Char {bar : Int}
    dict = Dict.fromList [('C', {bar = 2})]

    atC : Char -> Optic pr ls (Maybe {bar : Int}) (Maybe {bar : Int}) x y -> Lens ls (Dict Char {bar : Int}) (Dict Char {bar : Int}) x y
    atC =
        Dict.at_ String.fromChar

    get (atC 'C') dict
    --> Just {bar = 2}

    get (atC 'Z') dict
    --> Nothing

    try (atC 'C' << just_ << L.bar) dict
    --> Just 2

    set (atC 'C') Nothing dict
    --> Dict.remove 'C' dict

    set (atC 'Z' << just_ << L.bar) 3 dict
    --> dict
values.type: (comparable -> String.String) -> comparable -> Base.Optic pr ls (Maybe.Maybe a) (Maybe.Maybe a) x y -> Base.Lens ls (Dict.Dict comparable a) (Dict.Dict comparable a) x y

values.type: Base.Optic pr ls ( a, b ) c x y -> Base.Traversal (Dict.Dict a b) (Dict.Dict a c) x y

values.name: id
values.comment:
id: NON-structure preserving accessor over Dict's

In terms of accessors, think of Dicts as records where each field is a Maybe.

    import Accessors exposing (..)
    import Dict exposing (Dict)
    import Dict.Accessors as Dict
    import Lens as L

    dict : Dict Int {bar : Int}
    dict = Dict.fromList [(1, {bar = 2})]

    get (Dict.id 1) dict
    --> Just {bar = 2}

    get (Dict.id 0) dict
    --> Nothing

    try (Dict.id 1 << just_ << L.bar) dict
    --> Just 2

    set (Dict.id 1) Nothing dict
    --> Dict.remove 1 dict

    set (Dict.id 0 << just_ << L.bar) 3 dict
    --> dict
values.type: Basics.Int -> Base.Optic pr ls (Maybe.Maybe a) (Maybe.Maybe a) x y -> Base.Lens ls (Dict.Dict Basics.Int a) (Dict.Dict Basics.Int a) x y

values.name: at
values.comment:
at: Structure Preserving accessor over List members.

    import Accessors exposing (..)
    import List.Accessors as List
    import Lens as L

    list : List { bar : String }
    list = [{ bar = "Stuff" }, { bar =  "Things" }, { bar = "Woot" }]

    try (List.at 1) list
    --> Just { bar = "Things" }

    try (List.at 9000) list
    --> Nothing

    try (List.at 0 << L.bar) list
    --> Just "Stuff"

    set (List.at 0 << L.bar) "Whatever" list
    --> [{ bar = "Whatever" }, { bar =  "Things" }, { bar = "Woot" }]

    set (List.at 9000 << L.bar) "Whatever" list
    --> list
values.type: Basics.Int -> Base.Optic pr ls a a x y -> Base.Traversal (List.List a) (List.List a) x y

id: Structure Preserving accessor over List members.

    import Accessors exposing (..)
    import List.Accessors as List
    import Lens as L

    list : List { id : Int, bar : String }
    list = [{ id = 7, bar = "Stuff" }, { id = 1, bar =  "Things" }, { id = 5, bar = "Woot" }]

    try (List.id 1) list
    --> Just { id = 1, bar = "Things" }

    try (List.id 0) list
    --> Nothing

    try (List.id 7 << L.bar) list
    --> Just "Stuff"

    set (List.id 7 << L.bar) "Whatever" list
    --> [{ id = 7, bar = "Whatever" }, { id = 1, bar =  "Things" }, { id = 5, bar = "Woot" }]

    set (List.id 9000 << L.bar) "Whatever" list
    --> list
values.type: Basics.Int -> Base.Optic pr ls { a | id : Basics.Int } { a | id : Basics.Int } x y -> Base.Traversal (List.List { a | id : Basics.Int }) (List.List { a | id : Basics.Int }) x y

name: SelectList.Accessors
comment:
This module exposes some helpers for "miyamoen/select-list"

@docs each, each_, selected

values.name: each
values.type: Base.Optic pr ls a b x y -> Base.Traversal (SelectList.SelectList a) (SelectList.SelectList b) x y

values.name: each_
values.type: Base.Optic pr ls ( Basics.Int, a ) c x y -> Base.Traversal (SelectList.SelectList a) (SelectList.SelectList c) x y

values.name: selected
values.comment:
This accessor lets you traverse a list including the index of each element

    import Accessors exposing (..)
    import SelectList.Accessors as SL
    import Lens as L
    import SelectList exposing (SelectList)

    listRecord : { foo : SelectList { bar : Int } }
    listRecord =
        { foo = SelectList.fromLists [{ bar = 1 }] { bar = 2 } [{ bar = 3 }, { bar = 4 }]
        }

    multiplyIfGTOne : (Int, { bar : Int }) -> (Int, { bar : Int })
    multiplyIfGTOne ( idx, ({ bar } as rec) ) =
        if idx > 0 then
            ( idx, { bar = bar * 10 } )
        else
            (idx, rec)

    get (L.foo << SL.selected << L.bar) listRecord
    --> 2

    set (L.foo << SL.selected << L.bar) 37 listRecord
    --> { foo = SelectList.fromLists [{ bar = 1 }] { bar = 37 } [{ bar = 3 }, { bar = 4 }] }

    map (L.foo << SL.selected << L.bar) ((*) 10) listRecord
    --> { foo = SelectList.fromLists [{ bar = 1 }] { bar = 20 } [{ bar = 3 }, { bar = 4 }] }
values.type: Base.Optic pr ls b b x y -> Base.Lens ls (SelectList.SelectList b) (SelectList.SelectList b) x y

values.name: at
values.comment:
This accessor combinator lets you access the label at a particular TreePath.

    import Accessors exposing (..)
    import Tree exposing (Tree, tree)
    import Tree.Accessors as Tree
    import Tree.Extra.Lue exposing (leaf)
    import Lens as L

    simpleTree : Tree String
    simpleTree =
        tree "root"
            [ tree "a" [ leaf "b"]
            , tree "x" [ leaf "y"]
            ]

    try (Tree.at [0]) simpleTree
    --> Just "a"

    map (Tree.at [0]) String.toUpper simpleTree
    --> tree "root" [ tree "A" [ leaf "b" ] , tree "x" [ leaf "y" ] ]
values.type: TreePath.TreePath -> Base.Optic pr ls a a x y -> Base.Traversal (Tree.Tree a) (Tree.Tree a) x y

values.name: each
values.comment:
This accessor combinator lets you modify the type of each label of a Tree.

    import Accessors exposing (..)
    import Tree exposing (Tree, tree)
    import Tree.Accessors as Tree
    import Tree.Extra.Lue exposing (leaf)
    import Lens as L

    simpleTree : Tree String
    simpleTree =
        tree "r"
            [ tree "a" [ leaf "b"]
            , tree "x" [ leaf "y"]
            ]

    all Tree.each simpleTree
    --> ["r", "a", "b", "x", "y"]

    set Tree.each "1" simpleTree
    --> tree "1" [ tree "1" [ leaf "1" ] , tree "1" [ leaf "1" ] ]

    map Tree.each (\s -> "gimme an " ++ String.toUpper s ++ "!") simpleTree
    --> tree "gimme an R!" [ tree "gimme an A!" [ leaf "gimme an B!" ] , tree "gimme an X!" [ leaf "gimme an Y!" ] ]
values.type: Base.Optic pr ls a b x y -> Base.Traversal (Tree.Tree a) (Tree.Tree b) x y

values.name: label_
values.comment:
This accessor combinator lets you access the label of a given Tree.

    import Accessors exposing (..)
    import Tree exposing (Tree, tree)
    import Tree.Accessors as Tree
    import Tree.Extra.Lue exposing (leaf)
    import Lens as L

    simpleTree : Tree String
    simpleTree =
        tree "root"
            [ tree "a" [ leaf "b"]
            , tree "x" [ leaf "y"]
            ]

    get (Tree.label_) simpleTree
    --> "root"

    set (Tree.label_) "top 'o the mornin' to yah!" simpleTree
    --> tree "top 'o the mornin' to yah!" [ tree "a" [ leaf "b" ] , tree "x" [ leaf "y" ] ]

    map (Tree.label_) String.toUpper simpleTree
    --> tree "ROOT" [ tree "a" [ leaf "b" ] , tree "x" [ leaf "y" ] ]
values.type: Base.Optic pr ls a a x y -> Base.Lens ls (Tree.Tree a) (Tree.Tree a) x y

values.name: path
values.comment:
This accessor combinator lets you access a sub-tree at a given TreePath.

    import Accessors exposing (..)
    import Tree exposing (Tree, tree)
    import Tree.Accessors as Tree
    import Tree.Extra.Lue exposing (leaf)
    import Lens as L

    simpleTree : Tree String
    simpleTree =
        tree "root"
            [ tree "a" [ leaf "b"]
            , tree "x" [ leaf "y"]
            ]

    try (Tree.path [0,0]) simpleTree
    --> Just (leaf "b")

    set (Tree.path [1,0]) (tree "hey!" [leaf "deeper"]) simpleTree
    --> tree "root" [ tree "a" [ leaf "b" ] , tree "x" [ tree "hey!" [leaf "deeper"] ] ]

    map (Tree.path [0]) (Tree.mapLabel (\s -> "gimme an " ++ String.toUpper s ++ "!")) simpleTree
    --> tree "root" [ tree "gimme an A!" [ leaf "b" ] , tree "x" [ leaf "y" ] ]
values.type: TreePath.TreePath -> Base.Optic pr ls (Tree.Tree a) (Tree.Tree a) x y -> Base.Traversal (Tree.Tree a) (Tree.Tree a) x y

values.name: fst
values.comment:
Lens over the first component of a Tuple

    import Base exposing (get, set, map)
    import Tuple.Accessors as Tuple

    charging : (String, Int)
    charging = ("It's over", 1)

    get Tuple.fst charging
    --> "It's over"

    set Tuple.fst "It's over" charging
    --> ("It's over", 1)

    map Tuple.fst (\s -> String.toUpper s ++ "!!!") charging
    --> ("IT'S OVER!!!", 1)
values.type: Base.Optic pr ls a b x y -> Base.Lens ls ( a, two ) ( b, two ) x y

values.name: snd
values.comment:
import Base exposing (get, set, map)
    import Tuple.Accessors as Tuple

    meh : (String, Int)
    meh = ("It's over", 1)

    get Tuple.snd meh
    --> 1

    set Tuple.snd 1125 meh
    --> ("It's over", 1125)

    meh
        |> set Tuple.snd 1125
        |> map Tuple.fst (\s -> String.toUpper s ++ "!!!")
        |> map Tuple.snd ((*) 8)
    --> ("IT'S OVER!!!", 9000)
values.type: Base.Optic pr ls a b x y -> Base.Lens ls ( one, a ) ( one, b ) x y
